text: IRAN: Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei declares 'decisive victory' over US and Israel, announces 'new phase' in Hormuz Strait management. Says Iran 'will not forfeit its rights.' Statement broadcast on state TV.

sources.name: People's Daily — Iran supreme leader declares decisive victory
Al Jazeera — Khamenei says Tehran astonished the world

text: LEBANON: National mourning day declared after Israeli strikes kill 303 in single day (April 8) — deadliest day of Lebanon front. HRW condemns strikes, reports vital Qasmiyeh Bridge near Tyre destroyed, severing southern Lebanon from aid.

sources.name: IBTimes — Lebanon declares national mourning
HRW — Israeli strikes kill hundreds, damage vital bridge

text: UN: Russia and China VETO Security Council resolution on Strait of Hormuz freedom of navigation. UK expresses 'deep regret.' Veto protects Iran's leverage over strait during ceasefire negotiations.

sources.name: UN News — Russia and China veto resolution on Strait of Hormuz

text: TRUMP: Says Iran 'doing a very poor job' of letting oil through Hormuz despite ceasefire. Zero oil tankers have transited since truce. Remains 'very optimistic' about Islamabad peace talks Saturday with Vance delegation.

sources.name: Philstar — Trump says Iran doing poor job on Hormuz

text: LEBANON STRIKES: IDF continues hitting residential areas — Hanawya, Aita al-Shaab in south Lebanon struck. At least 7 killed. IDF 98th Division near-full control of Bint Jbeil. Pezeshkian says strikes 'violate temporary truce.'

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Israel strikes residential areas in southern Lebanon

text: HEZBOLLAH DEEP STRIKE: Missile targets Israeli naval base at Ashdod — ~90 miles from Lebanon border, one of Hezbollah's deepest attacks yet. Interceptors fired, no casualties. Hezbollah claims response to Israel's 'ceasefire violations.'

sources.name: Times of Israel — Hezbollah fires missile at Ashdod port
Al Arabiya — Hezbollah says it targeted Ashdod naval base

text: NABATIEH STRIKE: Israeli airstrike hits Nabatieh Serail government building — 13 Lebanese State Security personnel killed. Lebanon's President Aoun condemns attack on state institutions.

sources.name: AP via WSLS — Israeli strike kills Lebanese security forces
The Business Standard — 13 state security killed in Nabatieh

text: INFLATION: US CPI surges to 3.3% annually — gasoline prices up 21.2% in one month. War-driven oil shock is primary driver. Consumer sentiment at record low.

sources.name: CNN — Iran war oil shock pushes US inflation to 3.3%

text: CONGRESS: House GOP blocks Democratic effort to force vote on Iran war powers resolution. Speaker Pro Tempore gavels session closed without allowing debate.

sources.name: Democracy Now — House GOP blocks war powers vote

text: KHARAZI DEATH: Former Iranian FM Kamal Kharazi (81) dies from wounds sustained in April 1 US-Israeli airstrike on his Tehran home. Wife killed in same strike.

sources.name: Jerusalem Post — Kharazi dies from strike wounds

text: DIPLOMACY: Saudi FM holds first call with Iranian FM Araghchi since war began — discusses reducing tensions. Araghchi warns it would be 'dumb' for US to let Netanyahu 'kill diplomacy' via Lebanon strikes.

sources.name: Al Arabiya — Saudi FM call with Iranian counterpart
Al Jazeera — Iran says 'dumb' for US to let Netanyahu kill diplomacy

text: ISLAMABAD: Iranian delegation (Ghalibaf, Araghchi, SNSC Sec. Ahmadian) arrives at Nur Khan Airbase under aerial escort. Talks with US team (Vance, Witkoff, Kushner) set for Saturday at Serena Hotel. Pakistan PM Sharif: 'make-or-break moment.'

sources.name: IranWire — Iranian delegation arrives under aerial escort
Free Malaysia Today — Iranian delegation arrives in Pakistan

text: TRUMP WARNS: Tells NY Post warships 'loading up' with 'best ammunition ever made' — threatens fresh strikes if talks fail. Says military will hit Iran 'even better than what we did previously and we blew them apart.'

sources.name: Manila Times — Trump warns of fresh strikes if talks fail
Asharq Al-Awsat — Trump warns fresh strikes

text: PRECONDITIONS: Ghalibaf demands Lebanon ceasefire + release of frozen Iranian assets BEFORE Islamabad negotiations begin. Accuses US/Israel of 3 ceasefire violations: Lebanon attacks, drone in Iranian airspace, denial of enrichment rights.

sources.name: CNBC — Iran sets preconditions for talks
Daily Signal — Iran demands Lebanon ceasefire before talks

text: HORMUZ: Only 6 vessels transited April 10 (down from 7). 600+ vessels stranded including 325 tankers. UAE oil CEO: 'Hormuz is not open.' Russian-flagged tanker made it through. Blockade Day 39.

sources.name: NBC News — Hormuz shipping at standstill
The Week — 6 vessels passed through Hormuz

text: ISLAMABAD: VP Vance arrives in Pakistan to lead US delegation for Saturday peace talks with Iran. First face-to-face US-Iran negotiations since 1979. Iranian delegation already in place at Serena Hotel.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — US-Iran talks in Pakistan live
Euronews — Vance warns Iran

text: OIL: WTI drops sharply to $95.50 (-3.6% on day) as Brent-WTI spread normalizes. Brent at $96.69 (flat). Goldman warns $100+ Brent all year if Hormuz shut another month. JPMorgan: $150 if shut into mid-May.

sources.name: Trading Economics — Brent crude
CNBC — Oil prices

text: IMF: Warns of 'lasting economic damage' from Iran war — global growth permanently reduced. World Bank cuts Middle East growth to 1.8%. ECB warns of stagflation risk. S&P 500 best week since November (+3.6%).

sources.name: Euronews — IMF warns lasting economic damage

text: ISRAEL COSTS: Finance Ministry estimates $17.5B war costs for 40 days on Iran + Lebanon. Knesset approved record $45.8B defense budget + $13B supplemental. Up from $15B at 35 days.

sources.name: Middle East Monitor — Israel estimates $17.5B cost

text: SPR: DOE issues new RFP for 30M barrel emergency exchange from Strategic Petroleum Reserve. Previous 10M exchange April 1. Total IEA coordinated release: 400M barrels — largest in history.

sources.name: DOE — SPR emergency exchange

text: DC TALKS: Rubio organizing 3-way Israel-Lebanon-US talks in Washington for April 14. Lebanese PM Salam expected to visit next week. Israeli ambassador says Israel won't 'discuss ceasefire with Hezbollah.'

sources.name: CBS News — Lebanon Israel ceasefire talks in US
Axios — Israel-Lebanon negotiations

text: KIRYAT SHMONA: Hezbollah rocket hits building in northern Israel on Day 43 — continuing strikes despite Iran ceasefire. Lebanon front remains active with 5 IDF divisions deployed.

sources.name: LiveUAMap Lebanon — Building hit by Hezbollah missile

text: SAFED: Hezbollah fires 30+ rockets at northern Israel — direct hit on Safed parking lot, MDA ambulance damaged, 1,500-year-old Byzantine church in Nahariya hit. No injuries. Hezbollah claims retaliation for ceasefire violations.

sources.name: Jerusalem Post — Rocket hits parking lot in Safed
Haaretz — Hezbollah rockets cause heavy damage

text: LEBANON: Israeli strikes kill 13 on April 11 — 3 in Mefdoun dawn raid, 10 in Nabatiyeh including 3 emergency workers (1 civil defense, 2 Islamic Health Committee paramedics). 54+ emergency workers killed since March 2.

sources.name: NBC News — Live updates: Vance arrives for peace talks
Times of Israel — Liveblog April 11

text: HORMUZ MINES: US officials reveal Iran CANNOT FIND its own mines in Strait of Hormuz — drifted from original positions. Iran releases navigational maps (first mine acknowledgment). Clearance estimated 6-10 weeks. Only 9 ships transited Thursday.

sources.name: Japan Times — Iran unable to find mines in Hormuz
Jerusalem Post — Iran lost track of mine locations

text: ARAGHCHI: Iran enters Islamabad talks with 'complete distrust' toward US due to 'repeated breaches of commitment.' Warns Tehran could abandon ceasefire if Israeli strikes on Lebanon continue.

sources.name: PressTV — Iran enters talks with complete distrust

text: FROZEN ASSETS: Iran claims US agreed to release frozen assets in Qatar as 'sign of seriousness.' Senior US official denies it to CBS News. Dispute deepens mistrust ahead of Saturday talks.

sources.name: Al-Monitor — Iranian source says US agreed to release frozen assets

text: CPI: March 2026 inflation surges to 3.3% (from 2.4%). Gas prices +21.2% MoM — largest spike since 1967. Energy costs +10.9%. Consumer sentiment plummets to 47.6, lowest on record since 1952.

sources.name: CNN Business — CPI report: US inflation tripled
CNBC — CPI inflation March 2026 breakdown

text: HEGSETH DISPUTED: Army survivors from 103rd Sustainment Command contradict Defense Sec Hegseth's account of deadly Iranian strike in Kuwait. Survivors say base was unprepared; 'painting a picture that one squeaked through is a falsehood.'

sources.name: New Republic — Army survivors say Hegseth is lying

text: HISTORIC: First direct US-Iran face-to-face peace talks since 1979 begin in Islamabad. VP Vance leads US side; Ghalibaf and Araghchi lead Iran's 71-member delegation. Talks enter 'detailed, technical phase.' No agreements yet.

sources.name: NPR — U.S.-Iran peace talks underway in Islamabad
Al Jazeera — US-Iran direct talks on ending war under way in Pakistan

text: HORMUZ: USS Peterson and USS Murphy transit Strait of Hormuz — first US warships since war began. CENTCOM begins mine clearance. Iran calls it ceasefire violation, threatens attack 'within 30 minutes.'

sources.name: Axios — U.S. warships cross Strait of Hormuz for first time since Iran war began
USNI News — Two U.S. Warships Sail Through Strait of Hormuz

text: CHINA ARMS: CNN exclusive — US intelligence says China preparing to deliver MANPADs to Iran via third countries. Would mark major escalation in China's direct support. China denies.

sources.name: CNN — US intelligence: China preparing weapons shipment to Iran

text: TRUMP WARNING: '24 hours' to determine if Islamabad talks succeed. Warships being 'reloaded with the best ammunition.' Strikes could resume if negotiations fail.

sources.name: Military Times — US military begins clearing Strait of Hormuz, Trump says

text: LEBANON: Netanyahu formally rejects ceasefire with Hezbollah ahead of Washington talks Tuesday. IDF kills 10+ in southern Lebanon today. Lebanon toll: 2,020 killed, 6,436 wounded since March 2.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Israel rejects ceasefire with Hezbollah ahead of Lebanon talks

text: AL-AQSA: Over 100,000 worshippers at first Friday prayers after 40-day wartime closure. Mosque had been shut even during Ramadan.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Over 100,000 Muslim worshipers at Al-Aqsa
